pr.title: Attempt to resolve the mystery of HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty
pr.author: lukechampine

summary: Renamed ftCo_800C6AFC to HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty at 0x800C6AFC and centralized many previously fake TU-local HSD_JObjSet* helpers into baselib/jobj.h. The PR deleted several duplicate fake wrappers, removed gmregtyfall.static.h, switched many callers to real HSD_JObjSet* helpers where possible, and added explicit WithMtxDirty variants for cases that still needed the direct dirty-call behavior for matching. Review discussion notes that the parenthesized function call form was odd but relevant to macro/function behavior, and that the macro remains fake; the cleanup was accepted as an incremental improvement.
what_changed: Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt and scratches.txt so the function at 0x800C6AFC is named HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty instead of ftCo_800C6AFC.
Changed the actual definition in src/melee/ft/ft_0C31.c from ftCo_800C6AFC to void (HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty)(HSD_JObj* jobj), and removed the ftCo_800C6AFC prototype from ft_0C31.h.
Added a HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty declaration and many WithMtxDirty inline variants to src/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.h, including rotation, scale, translate, and add helpers.
Added HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtyOutOfLineLeaf, HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtyOutOfLine, and HSD_JObjSetTranslateWithMtxDirtyOutOfLine to force an out-of-line call shape in specific matching cases.
Deleted src/melee/gm/gmregtyfall.static.h, which had contained copied fake JObj helpers and ftCo_800C6AFC dontinline wrappers.

Replaced fake_tyfall_HSD_JObjSetTranslateX/Y/Z, fake_tyfall_HSD_JObjSetRotationY, and fake_tyfall_HSD_JObjSetScaleX/Y/Z in gmregtyfall.c and gm_1A4C.c with corresponding HSD_JObj*WithMtxDirty helpers.
Removed Kirby-local fake JObj helpers in ftKb_SpecialNNs.c and replaced calls with real HSD_JObjGetScale, HSD_JObjSetScale, and HSD_JObjSetRotationY where matching allowed.
Replaced fake item/stage JObj helpers in multiple item and stage files with centralized HSD_JObj*WithMtxDirty helpers or direct parenthesized HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty calls.
Reduced duplicate inline code across multiple TUs while preserving matching-sensitive dirty-matrix call behavior.
smart_moves: Identified that the supposedly fighter-local ftCo_800C6AFC was actually HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty emitted in the fighter range due to inclusion/linker/order behavior rather than a true ft subsystem function.
Moved repeated fake helpers into jobj.h instead of continuing to clone TU-local implementations with custom names and duplicated assert line numbers.
Kept normal HSD_JObjSet* helpers for callers that match with the real helper behavior, while introducing explicit WithMtxDirty variants only for call sites needing the direct dirty-call shape.
Used a parenthesized function name, (HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty)(jobj), to avoid macro-style expansion differences and obtain distinct behavior from an unwrapped macro call.
Created out-of-line leaf wrapper helpers to reproduce call emission in ft_0C31.c without keeping ad hoc please_dontinline/dontinline wrappers tied to the old ftCo_800C6AFC name.
Deleted an entire static header of fake gmregtyfall JObj wrappers, reducing local decomp hacks and centralizing the workaround.
decomp_lessons: A symbol appearing in an unexpected range can be an inlined or emitted header function body rather than evidence that the function belongs to that subsystem.
Before keeping fake TU-local helpers, check whether their body is a known baselib inline whose out-of-line emission landed in the first call-site TU.
For header inlines, linker order and the first non-inlined call site can explain why a baselib helper appears in a fighter object range.
When replacing fake helpers, preserve assert file and line behavior from the original header if those asserts affect matching.
Centralized WithMtxDirty helpers are preferable to many file-specific fake_HSD_JObjSet* helpers, but their names should make the matching workaround explicit.
Parenthesizing a function-like macro name at a call site can suppress macro invocation and produce different compiler behavior; this can be useful but should be treated as suspicious and documented.
If full unification is not possible, an incremental cleanup that removes duplicate fakes and labels the remaining workaround is still valuable.
naming_conventions: Use HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty for the real 0x800C6AFC symbol instead of the misleading ftCo_800C6AFC name.
Use HSD_JObjSet*WithMtxDirty for helper variants that directly call HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty rather than the normal header behavior.
Use HSD_JObjSetTranslateWithMtxDirtyOutOfLine when the matching requirement is specifically an out-of-line dirty call.

Avoid file-local names like fake_tyfall_HSD_JObjSetTranslateX, fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslate, ftKb_JObjSetScale, and ftCo_800C6AFC_dontinline once a centralized baselib helper can express the same intent.
Parenthesized symbol spelling, e.g. void (HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty)(HSD_JObj* jobj) and (HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty)(jobj), was used to distinguish function identity from macro expansion behavior.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Renaming ftCo_800C6AFC to HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty preserved the address 0x800C6AFC while correcting semantic ownership.
For some call sites, replacing fake helpers with normal HSD_JObjSetScale, HSD_JObjGetScale, or HSD_JObjSetRotationY was enough to match.
For call sites that previously routed through ftCo_800C6AFC, WithMtxDirty variants directly call (HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty)(jobj) after updating rotation, scale, or translation fields.
HSD_JObjSetTranslateWithMtxDirtyOutOfLine routes through HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtyOutOfLine and HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtyOutOfLineLeaf to influence call/inlining shape.
The dirty check pattern remains guarded by JOBJ_MTX_INDEP_SRT in the setters, matching existing HSD_JObj helper semantics.
The PR explicitly relied on parenthesized function calls to alter macro behavior; reviewer discussion confirmed this was unusual but relevant.
The diff preserves jobj.h assert line numbers in centralized helper variants, which is important for matching code that emits assert metadata.
review_feedback: Reviewer ribbanya approved but asked why the called function was wrapped in parentheses.

Reviewer noted a plausible explanation: if the function lives in jobj.h, and the first call site based on linker order is in ft_0C31.c and is not inlined, the compiler may emit the function body in that TU.
Author later observed that wrapping a macro in parentheses when calling it results in different behavior than calling it unwrapped.
Reviewer commented that the macro is for sure fake, but was fine merging the cleaned-up duplicates for now even with the weird parentheses.
No line-level review comments were present in the provided slice.
follow_up_queries: Find remaining fake_HSD_JObjSet* or fake_tyfall_HSD_JObjSet* helpers that could be replaced by centralized jobj.h helpers.
Audit all uses of parenthesized (HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty) to determine whether a cleaner non-macro symbol arrangement can preserve matching.
Investigate whether the WithMtxDirty variants are temporary matching shims or should be replaced once HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty macro/function behavior is better understood.
Search for other baselib header functions emitted into unexpected subsystem ranges due to first call site and linker order.
Review assert line-number preservation in newly centralized jobj.h helper variants when future jobj.h edits shift line numbers or macros.
